content.problemStatement:
Given two arrays of nuts and bolts of equal size NNN, where each nut has a unique matching bolt. You need to match all nuts and bolts efficiently. The comparison can only be made between a nut and a bolt, not between two nuts or two bolts. Elements should follow the order: { !,#,$,%,&,*,?,@,^ }


Input Format

First line contains TTT, the number of test cases
For each test case:

First line contains NNN
Second line contains NNN characters
Third line contains NNN characters




Output Format

For each test case, print two lines:

First line: matched nuts array
Second line: matched bolts array
constraints: All characters are from the set {'!','#','$','%','&','*','?','@','^'}
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains an integer N, the number of nuts and bolts. The second line contains N characters separated by spaces representing the nuts. The third line contains N characters separated by spaces representing the bolts. All characters belong to the set {'!','#','$','%','&','*','?','@','^'} and each appears at most once in each array.
content.outputFormat: For each test case print two lines. The first line is the nuts array sorted according to the fixed order ! < # < $ < % < & < * < ? < @ < ^, characters separated by a single space. The second line is the bolts array sorted in the same order, also space‑separated.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Both arrays are reordered according to the fixed order ! < # < $ < % < & < * < ? < @ < ^. After sorting, nuts and bolts become identical.
Sorting the three symbols yields the sequence ! # $ for both nuts and bolts.
After applying the custom order, the sorted sequence is ! # $ % & @ ^ for both arrays.

solution.approach: The only operation allowed is comparing a nut with a bolt, but since the matching is one‑to‑one and the order of symbols is fixed, we can simply sort both arrays using the predefined order. We map each symbol to its rank (0‑8) and sort the character arrays with a comparator based on this rank. After sorting, the nuts and bolts arrays are identical and correctly ordered.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case for sorting, where N ≤ 9, so it is effectively O(N). Total O(T * N log N).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) auxiliary space for the character arrays.
